desc: Matsumoto pairs black-walled Matsumoto Castle and merchant-era streets with a dramatic Japanese Alps backdrop, so the city shifts from blossom and fresh-green hiking season to crisp foliage and snowy onsen-town escapes.
hls: Feudal-era Matsumoto Castle framed by the Northern Alps skyline
Kamikōchi and alpine trail access in the Central Japanese Alps
Living craft/food culture in Nakamachi kura storehouses and Nawate lanes
Clear four-season identity: blossoms, summer highlands, autumn color, winter hot springs

todo.name: Kamikōchi river-trail day
todo.desc: Weather-dependent: go on clear days for mountain views and safer alpine walks (seasonal bus access).

todo.name: Matsumoto Castle keep and moat circuit
todo.desc: All-weather option: the castle interior and nearby museums work well even in rain or shoulder-season cold.

todo.name: Nakamachi + Nawate heritage stroll
todo.desc: Mix kura warehouses, craft shops, and short café stops between riverside lanes.

todo.name: Japan Ukiyo-e Museum
todo.desc: All-weather option with one of Japan's deepest ukiyo-e print collections.

todo.name: Asama Onsen evening soak
todo.desc: Ideal in colder months after walking days; many baths stay open in poor weather.

todo.name: Shinshu soba and miso tasting
todo.desc: Sample regional noodle styles and miso-forward cooking around the station and old town.

wifi.note: Strong in city hotels and station-area cafés; mountain lodges vary.

prac.fltNote: For UK/Ireland arrivals, Haneda (HND) and Narita (NRT) are usually the most reliable long-haul entry points; continue to Matsumoto by limited domestic sectors to MMJ or, more commonly, by JR rail via Tokyo/Shinjuku, with strongest booking pressure around sakura (late Mar-Apr), Golden Week (late Apr-early May), Obon (mid-Aug), and autumn foliage weekends.
prac.lang: Japanese is the primary language; English support is best at transport hubs, hotels, and major attractions.
prac.tz: JST (UTC+9) year-round; no daylight saving time.
prac.visa: Japan entry is nationality-dependent; many short-stay tourists are visa-exempt, while others need a visa issued before departure.
alerts: Kamikōchi access roads close in winter and can face shoulder-season weather delays.
Castle and alpine bus queues surge on Japanese long weekends; reserve transport early.
Winter mornings are icy—use traction-friendly footwear for old-town lanes.

sweet: Late May-June and October are the usual sweet spots: better hiking and city-walk weather than winter, but lower price/crowd pressure than Golden Week, Obon, and peak foliage weekends.
